auth.loginSubtitle: Sign in to your BitBonsai account

setup.welcomeSubtitle: Let's get you set up in just a few steps
setup.nodeTypeSubtitle: Is this a main node or child node?
setup.mainNodeDesc: The primary node that manages the database and coordinates encoding jobs
setup.childNodeDesc: A worker node that performs encoding tasks
setup.allowWithoutAuth: Allow local network access without authentication
setup.tokenDesc: Enter this token on your main node to connect

libraries.title: Libraries
libraries.clickAddButton: Click the "Add Your First Library" button below
libraries.chooseName: Choose a name for your library (e.g., "Movies", "TV Shows")
libraries.selectFolder: Select the folder path containing your media files
libraries.pickNode: Pick a node to handle the encoding work
libraries.enableWatching: Enable file watching to automatically detect new files
libraries.librariesDescription: Libraries are collections of media files that BitBonsai monitors and optimizes.

policies.title: Policies
policies.subtitle: Create and manage encoding policy templates
policies.noPoliciesHint: Policies define how your media files should be encoded.
policies.name: Name
policies.policyNameHint: Give your policy a memorable name
policies.selectDevices: Select which devices should play your encoded files
policies.startWithBalanced: Start with "Balanced HEVC" for a great mix of
policies.qualityHint: Lower values = better quality + larger files.

nodes.title: Nodes
nodes.openTerminal: Open a terminal on the machine you want to use as a node
nodes.pasteCommand: Paste and run the command to install the node software
nodes.nodeAppear: Your node will appear here and start accepting encoding jobs!
nodes.createRegistrationRequest: On your child node, create a registration request via the discovery page
nodes.generateToken: The system will generate a 6-digit pairing token
nodes.copyToken: Copy the token and paste it here
nodes.tokenValid: The token is valid for 24 hours
nodes.alternativeWait: Alternative: You can also wait for the child node to send a registration request, which will appear in your pending requests.

insights.title: Insights
insights.subtitle: Analytics and performance metrics
insights.processFiles: Process files to see codec distribution
insights.addNodes: Add worker nodes to see performance metrics

errors.generic: An error occurred. Please try again.
errors.network: Network error. Please check your connection.

storage.title: Storage Configuration Wizard
storage.option1: Option 1: Enable privileged mode on the LXC container
storage.option2: Option 2: Mount NFS on the Proxmox host and bind-mount into container
storage.option3: Option 3: Use rsync file transfer (automatic, no setup needed)
storage.step1Title: Step 1: Enable NFS on Main Node
storage.securityNote: Security Note: Privileged mode gives the container root access.
storage.createSharesDesc: BitBonsai will create and mount NFS shares automatically.
storage.manualItem5: Click Mount on each detected share
storage.jobAssigned: Job Assigned: When a job is assigned to {{node}}
storage.fileTransfer: File Transfer: Source file is transferred via rsync (~1-2 min for 10GB)
storage.encoding: Encoding: FFmpeg processes the file locally on {{node}}
storage.resultTransfer: Result Transfer: Encoded file is transferred back to {{source}}
storage.cleanup: Cleanup: Temp files are automatically removed
storage.enableRsyncDesc: Set the target node to use file transfers:
storage.testingRsyncDesc: Verifying SSH access and file transfer...
storage.testEncodingDesc: Run a small test job on {{node}}...

storageShares.title: Manage NFS/SMB network storage for this node

nodeStats.title: Node Statistics
nodeStats.noLibrariesDesc: This node is not currently managing any media libraries

scheduleConflict.title: Schedule Conflict Warning
scheduleConflict.description: The job will be assigned to this node, but it may not start encoding until the node's schedule window begins.
scheduleConflict.outsideWindow: {{nodeName}} is currently outside its scheduled encoding window.

cpuCapacity.title: CPU Capacity Management
cpuCapacity.subtitle: Intelligent resource allocation for optimal performance

jobDelegation.outsideSchedule: This node is currently outside its encoding schedule window

filesDialog.noFilesFound: No files found matching the criteria.

addFiles.title: Add Files to Queue
addFiles.selectLibrary: Select which library's files to add
addFiles.librariesOptimized: All libraries are fully optimized or no libraries are configured.
addFiles.pleaseWait: Please wait while we add your selected files to the queue...

libraryFiles.title: Library Files
libraryFiles.mayTakeMinutes: This may take a few minutes for large libraries
libraryFiles.noFilesMatch: No files match your search term
libraryFiles.noVideoFiles: This library doesn't contain any video files.

connectionError.title: Unable to Connect to Server
connectionError.checkServerRunning: Check if the server is running
connectionError.serverRunningDesc: Make sure the BitBonsai server application is started and running
connectionError.networkDesc: Ensure your device is connected to the same network as the server
connectionError.configDesc: Verify the server is listening on the correct port and address
connectionError.firewallDesc: Ensure your firewall allows connections to the BitBonsai server
connectionError.docsLink: For detailed setup instructions, please refer to the BitBonsai documentation

nodeSchedule.addTimeDesc: Click and drag on the calendar to create a window
nodeSchedule.resizeDesc: Drag the edges of a window to adjust start/end times
nodeSchedule.moveDesc: Drag a window to change its day or time
nodeSchedule.removeDesc: Click any window to delete it

library.nameMaxLength: Name must be less than 255 characters
library.pathHint: Absolute path to the media folder on the node filesystem
library.pathAbsolute: Path must be an absolute path (start with /)
library.contentType: Type of media content in this library
library.scanEnabledDesc: This library will be scanned for media files and encoding jobs will be created based on your policies.
library.scanDisabledDesc: The library is completely ignored by BitBonsai - no scanning, no jobs, no processing. Use this to temporarily pause a library without deleting it.
library.watchEnabledDesc: BitBonsai automatically detects new files added to this library in real-time using file system monitoring (inotify).
library.watchDisabledDesc: You need to manually click "Scan" to detect new files. This uses less system resources but requires manual action.

nodeConfig.title: Configure Node
nodeConfig.nodeNameMinLength: Node name must be at least 1 character
nodeConfig.concurrentJobsHelp: Number of concurrent encoding jobs this node can handle
nodeConfig.maxWorkersMin: Max workers must be at least 1
nodeConfig.cpuLimitHelp: Maximum CPU usage to reserve resources for host system
nodeConfig.cpuLimitHighWarning: High CPU usage may impact host system performance
nodeConfig.cpuLimitMin: CPU limit must be at least 10%
nodeConfig.scheduleHelp: Define time windows when this node is allowed to encode (24/7 if disabled)

nodeSetup.connectDescription: Let's connect this child node to your BitBonsai main node. Choose how you want to connect.
nodeSetup.howToConnect: How would you like to connect to your main node?
nodeSetup.autoDiscoverDesc: Automatically scan your local network to find the main node
nodeSetup.manualCodeDesc: Enter a 6-digit pairing code from your main node
nodeSetup.enterPairingCodeDesc: Enter the 6-digit pairing code from your main node
nodeSetup.pairingCodeHint: Get this code from your main node at Nodes → Add Child Node
nodeSetup.leaveEmptyAutoDetect: Leave empty to auto-detect from the network
nodeSetup.scanningDescription: Looking for BitBonsai main nodes on your local network...
nodeSetup.selectMainNodeDesc: Choose which main node you want to connect to:
nodeSetup.childNodeName: Give this child node a name:
nodeSetup.childNodeNameHint: This name will identify this node in the main node's dashboard
nodeSetup.enterCodeOnMain: Enter this code on the main node to approve the connection
nodeSetup.waitingForApproval: Waiting for approval from main node...

storageShares2.autoDetectLinked: Click "Auto-Detect Shares" to discover shares from the main node, or add a share manually.
storageShares2.autoDetectMain: Add a storage share to make files accessible to other nodes without file transfers.

libraryCard.defaultPolicyTooltip: Files in this library will be encoded using the '{{name}}' policy by default. You can override this when creating jobs.
libraryCard.noDefaultPolicyTooltip: This library has no default encoding policy configured. You'll need to manually select a policy each time you create encoding jobs.
libraryCard.clickToEnable: Click to enable automatic file detection

libraryForm.defaultPolicyHint: The default policy will be automatically selected when creating encoding jobs for this library. You can always override it when creating jobs manually.

queue2.subtitle: Manage job queue and encoding tasks
queue2.noCompletedYet: No jobs have completed yet. Jobs will appear here once they finish encoding successfully.
queue2.noEncodingNow: No jobs are currently encoding. Jobs will appear here when encoding starts.
queue2.noPausedJobs: No jobs are paused. Jobs appear here when you manually pause an encoding job.
queue2.noQueuedJobs: No jobs are waiting in the queue. Add files or scan libraries to create new jobs.
queue2.noFailedJobs: No jobs have failed. This is good! Failed jobs will appear here if encoding errors occur.
queue2.noCancelledJobs: No jobs have been cancelled. Cancelled jobs will appear here if you manually stop any jobs.
queue2.noDetectedJobs: No jobs are in detected state. Jobs appear here briefly when first discovered before health checks.
queue2.noHealthCheckJobs: No jobs are being health checked. Jobs appear here briefly while file integrity is verified.
queue2.noVerifyingJobs: No jobs are being verified. Jobs appear here briefly after encoding to validate the output.
queue2.noSearchResults: No jobs match your search query "{{query}}". Try a different search term.
queue2.noNodeJobs: No jobs are assigned to the selected node. Try selecting a different node or "All Nodes".
queue2.noLibraryJobs: No jobs are assigned to the selected library. Try selecting a different library or "All Libraries".
queue2.noFilterMatch: No jobs match your current filters. Try adjusting your filters or clearing them.
queue2.noJobsDescription: The queue shows all your encoding jobs - past, present, and future. Jobs are created automatically when BitBonsai finds media files that need optimization.
queue2.step1: First, set up a Library to tell BitBonsai where your media files are
queue2.step2: Create a Policy to define how files should be encoded
queue2.step3: Register a Node to do the actual encoding work
queue2.step4: Scan your library or add files - jobs will appear here automatically!
queue2.step5: Watch as your nodes process jobs and optimize your media
queue2.tip: Jobs start automatically once you have libraries, policies, and nodes configured!
queue2.noPreviewsYet: No previews yet. Click "Capture Now" to create one.
queue2.cancelDialogMessage: This job will be stopped and removed from the queue. Choose what happens next:
queue2.whyFlaggedDesc: These files are already encoded in the target codec specified by the policy. This typically happens when:
queue2.flaggedReason1: The policy's target codec was changed after jobs were created
queue2.flaggedReason2: Files were already optimized and re-added to the queue
queue2.skipResult1: Jobs will be marked as COMPLETED
queue2.skipResult4: No disk space will be saved (files are already optimal)
queue2.whyForceReEncodeDesc: These files are already in the target codec. Re-encoding is useful when:
queue2.forceReason1: You want to re-compress at a different quality (CRF) setting
queue2.forceReason2: You want to change the container format
queue2.forceReason3: You want to apply different encoding flags
queue2.forceWarning1: Re-encoding the same codec can increase file size if quality settings are similar
queue2.forceWarning2: Each re-encode causes generational quality loss
queue2.forceWarning3: This will consume significant CPU/GPU time
queue2.retryAllCancelledDesc: All {{count}} cancelled job(s) will be moved back to the queue and processed by available encoding nodes. The estimated time is based on an average encoding speed of {{speed}}/s and assumes continuous processing.
queue2.policyNote: Jobs will use their original encoding policy settings
queue2.retryFilteredDesc: {{count}} failed job(s) with the selected error will be moved back to the queue and processed by available encoding nodes.
queue2.retryAllFailedDesc: All {{count}} failed job(s) will be moved back to the queue and processed by available encoding nodes.
queue2.filterDescription: Select a specific error to retry only those jobs, or leave unselected to retry all failed jobs.
queue2.cancelAllConfirm: Are you sure you want to cancel ALL queued jobs?
queue2.cancelAllWarning: This will cancel {{count}} job(s). All cancelled jobs can be retried later.
queue2.clearJobsConfirmStages: Are you sure you want to permanently delete all {{stages}} jobs?
queue2.clearJobsConfirmAll: Are you sure you want to permanently delete ALL jobs?
queue2.clearJobsWarning: This action cannot be undone! Job records will be permanently removed from the database.
queue2.clearJobsNote: Note: This will NOT delete your actual media files, only the job records.
